title: OCR PDF — Make Scans Searchable Offline
h1: Extract and Recognize Text from Scanned PDFs
description: Run OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable and copyable, entirely in your browser using Tesseract. No upload. Great for users in Moscow.

sections.title: Why Run OCR Locally?
sections.content: Scanned PDFs contain sensitive text. Running OCR on a cloud service exposes that text to a third party. Ours runs 100% offline.

steps.title: Load Scanned PDF
steps.description: Select the image-based or scanned PDF.

steps.title: Select Language
steps.description: Choose the document language for better accuracy.

steps.title: Download Searchable PDF
steps.description: Get a text-layer PDF ready to search and copy.

sections.title: OCR Tool for Moscow Archives
sections.content: Government agencies and businesses in Moscow digitize paper archives using our offline OCR tool.

sections.title: Scanned Content Never Exposed
sections.content: OCR processing using Tesseract WASM runs entirely in browser memory — no API calls, no data transmission.

sections.title: OCR Any Document Locally
sections.content: Process a 20-page scanned PDF in under 30 seconds. All Tesseract processing happens in-browser.

faqs.a: Accuracy depends on scan quality; clear, high-contrast scans yield 95%+ accuracy.

faqs.q: Does it create a searchable PDF?
faqs.a: Yes, a text layer is added making the PDF fully searchable.

faqs.q: Can I copy text from the result?
faqs.a: Yes, the output PDF has selectable, copyable text.
